channel.title: GrantOtter — Federal Funding Updates
channel.description: Aggregated weekly feed of grant opportunities and agency news from NIH, NSF, and DARPA.

item.title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
item.description: NASA researchers recently put a new wing design, appearing long and thin with a lightweight structural design, through a series of grueling tests to find its structural limits. What they found left them encouraged about the wing’s potential, even when they pushed it past its intended limits. The 15-foot Structural Wing Experiment Evaluating Truss-bracing (SWEET-15) […]

item.title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
item.description: Description This composite of images taken by NASA’s Psyche mission shows the crescent of Mars grow as the spacecraft approached the planet for a gravity assist from May 2 to May 15, 2026. The series begins with the smallest crescent at the center of the of the image as Mars is farthest from the spacecraft, and progressively […]

item.title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
item.description: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) initiated a working group to review updated VTE case information, additional data gathered revealing altered blood flow status within a cohort of astronauts, and discuss progress of research and clinical activities intended to mitigate the risk of VTE during spaceflight with new […]
item.category: Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO)

item.title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
item.description: CONTRACT RELEASE NASA has selected Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC to provide comprehensive operations, maintenance, and repair services for NASA’s Ames Research Center in California’s Silicon Valley.  The Ames Facilities Support Services II contract ensures that the center’s historic and specialized facilities are properly maintained, fully operational, and capable of supporting the agency’s missions and tenant partners in the NASA Resea

item.title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
item.description: This composite image, released on July 9, 2026, shows the region around a pulsar – a neutron star with a strong magnetic field that spins incredibly fast – within the Lighthouse nebula. The image contains X-ray data from NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory in purple, X-rays from NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) in blue, and […]

item.title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
item.description: Read this release in English here. Ya está abierto el plazo de acreditación de los medios de comunicación para el lanzamiento de la misión del telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la NASA. El lanzamiento del telescopio Roman está programado para no antes de las 7:20 a.m. (hora del este) del domingo 30 de agosto, […]

item.title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
item.description: Media accreditation now is open for the launch of NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the agency’s SpaceX Crew-13 missions, both targeting launch in the coming months. The Roman telescope is slated to launch no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT Sunday, Aug. 30, aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A at […]

item.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
item.description: Description Captured by the multispectral imager instrument on NASA’s Psyche mission, this is an enhanced-color mosaic created from four individual images acquired on May 15, 2026, during the spacecraft’s flyby of Mars. Psyche was traveling from right to left (northeast to southwest on Mars) during the six minutes that it took to acquire the images for […]

item.title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
item.description: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the global coalition committed to responsible space exploration, becoming the newest signatory and seventh African country to join the Artemis Accords. NASA’s Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson contributed video remarks for a signing ceremony on Friday, in the island nation’s city of Ébène. “We are honored to welcome Mauritius […]
item.category: Office of International and Interagency Relations (OIIR)

item.title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
item.description: The expansive mudflats, sandy beaches, and mangrove forests of the Bijagós archipelago support an array of migratory shorebirds and large numbers of sea turtles.

item.title: Notice of NCATS Participation in Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) PA-25-302 NIH Small Research Grant Program (Parent R03 Clinical Trial Not Allowed)
item.description: Notice NOT-TR-26-011 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of NCATS Participation in Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) PA-25-304 NIH Exploratory/Developmental Research Project Grant (Parent R21 Clinical Trial Not Allowed)
item.description: Notice NOT-TR-26-010 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Perrseus
item.description: DARPA is soliciting innovative proposals that enable compact cold-atom devices and environmentally-decoupled ultra-high Q microelectromechanical and photonics devices through microscale ultra-high-vacuum systems (µUHV). | See Program

item.title: DARPA and U.S. Air Force fly AI-controlled F-16, paving the way for autonomous air combat
item.description: Historic VENOM milestone demonstrates scalable AI development capabilities for the operational fleet.

item.title: Quickly Learn about NIH Grants and Funding through the New NIH Innovates Social Channels
item.description: As part of NIH’s ongoing efforts to modernize its web presence and make information easier to find in one place, our NIH Funding social media accounts have transitioned to NIH Innovates on X and LinkedIn. You can expect the same information on grants and funding, plus more updates from NIH’s institutes, centers, and offices.

item.title: Updates to NIH Fellowship, Training, and Career Development Applications: Referral and Review Highlights
item.description: If you’re planning to apply for an NIH fellowship (F), career development award (K), or training grant (T), staying informed about recent NIH efforts to centralize peer review can help you understand how your application may be assigned and reviewed.

item.title: Podcast: New security and communications materials
item.description: Nicholas Kotov discusses complex materials and the development of new fused-silk materials with applications in security and communications...

item.title: Notice of NIMH Participation in PA-25-080 "NIH Support for Conferences and Scientific Meetings (Parent R13 Clinical Trial Not Allowed)"
item.description: Notice NOT-MH-26-045 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: NSF awards 12 new Regional Innovation Engines to fuel research, jobs and economic growth nationwide
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced it awarded the newest NSF Regional Innovation Engines (NSF Engines) awards to 12 U.S. teams across 20 states. These NSF Engines will build and scale innovation clusters that aim to accelerate the…

item.title: Extramural Loan Repayment Program for Facilitating Opportunities in Cutting-edge Science (LRP-FOCUS)
item.description: Notice NOT-OD-26-083 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Extramural Loan Repayment Program for Pediatric Research (LRP-PR)
item.description: Notice NOT-OD-26-082 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Extramural Loan Repayment Program for Clinical Researchers (LRP-CR)
item.description: Notice NOT-OD-26-081 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts
